Document:
# Dwarves
Dwarves or Dwarfs are a stout, mostly hairless species from the planet Abumas, located near the Null Scar. Their convergent evolution toward a Human-like appearance is strong enough to result in frequent misidentification, though there are enough deep cultural and physiological differences to justify treating them as a distinct group, outside the gamut of other near-Human subspecies like half-Felionoids and half-Vulpkanin, in addition to their unique origin as a totally separate clade.
Most Dwarves are male (around 87%), with the rest being female. Gender-nonconforming Dwarves are rarer than their Humans counterparts. Male Dwarves are often easily identified by their full, shaggy hair and large beards. Dwarves with no hair, including beardless males, are generally considered unusual.
The average Dwarf is around 110 cm (3' 7") in height at maturity, with no significant difference between the sexes, but exhibits much fuller, denser bones and pronounced cartilaginous features compared to humans such as larger noses and cheekbones. This robustness grants them the ability to endure [color=#40ff40]10% more damage[/color] than humans before succumbing to their injuries. Xenobiologists widely agree that Dwarves evolved this stature to withstand the enhanced gravity of Abumas, which is around 1.3 times Earth standard.
Dwarf society has many nuances and subdivisions, and no two Dwarf settlements are truly the same, but ethnographers have long noted a general tendency toward certain parallels with the Nordic, Celtic, and Germanic cultures of Earth in terms of their material culture. The primary weapon wielded by Dwarves during their Iron Age was the battle-ax, followed by the war-pick and war-hammer, with relatively little emphasis on spears, bows, and swords; surviving specimens tend to be highly engraved with knot patterns and runes. The major Dwarf ethnic groups are named according to their favored biomes: Subterranean Dwarves, Deepdwarves, Steppedwarves, Oceandwarves, Spiredwarves, Mountain Dwarves, Jungle Dwarves, and Forest Dwarves.
Dwarves have long been a space-faring race. Their homeworld of Abumas lacks significant rare earth mineral deposits near the surface, resulting in a monopoly on these resources by the Deepdwarves. To break this monopoly, they surface-dwelling Dwarves began mining asteroids and planetoids as a workaround. These space settlements, usually called Citadels, begin their lives as simple mining outposts. As they attract more families and workers, the outposts, homes, and businesses, expand into the shafts and tunnels, eventually forming splendid cities with diverse economies and populations. A fully-developed Citadel is a massive ark that can fly under its own power, though the majority of asteroid mines are abandoned once the minerals run out and never achieve this glory. Particularly impressive or culturally significant Citadels are often landed on planets to serve as museums or palaces, and there is a thriving industry of using grounded Citadels to stake a claim on newly-colonized worlds, as a 'readymade city' that can be deployed in an afternoon.
Abumas's proximity to the Null Scar inevitably meant that Dwarves would come in contact with NanoTrasen. Several Citadels now have highly profitable relationships with NT, and are under direct contract to provide certain high-demand minerals to the far-flung corners of Starlight Sector, beyond the reach of the Corporation's logistical network.
## Dwarf Alchemy To most species, alcohol is a poisonous but highly entertaining beverage valued for its euphoric and sedative effects. This is only partly true for Dwarves, who have a [color=#1e90ff]physiological need for alcohol[/color], much like Humans need dietary fat to remain healthy; it is a critical macronutrient. To a Dwarf, [color=#1e90ff]alcoholic drinks also satiate hunger[/color], and [color=#1e90ff]food is less effective at satiating hunger[/color]. Moreover, [color=#ff901e]pure water is intolerable and causes vomiting[/color].
More importantly, Dwarves often have odd reactions to certain beverages. Some of these peculiar interactions are described below.
The following drinks grant benefits directly from the tap, without any further mixing:
Water
equivalent to Ipecac (causes vomiting)
Ale
restores thirst; no Ethanol
Beer
restores hunger
Mead
equivalent to Tricordazine
Vodka
equivalent to Hyronalin
Whiskey
10% less Blunt damage taken
Rum
10% less Slash damage taken
Absinthe
10% less Piercing damage taken
Tequila
10% less Heat damage taken
Cognac
10% less Cold damage taken
Gin
10% less Caustic damage taken
Vermouth
10% less Shock damage taken
### Easy Brew These drinks require a small amount of work to mix.
Antifreeze
20% less Cold damage taken
Manly Dorf
equivalent to Bicardine
Iced Beer
equivalent to Dermaline and Kelotane
Red Mead
equivalent to Saline and Dexalin
White Gilgamesh
equivalent to Dylovene
Martini
equivalent to Potassium Iodide
Tequila Sunrise
equivalent to Tranexamic Acid
Dark and Stormy
heals Shock damage
Death in the Afternoon
raises critical threshold to 129; death threshold is reduced to 130
Manhattan
immune to slippery surfaces
Brave Bull
punches deal Structure damage
Irish Cream
attack 25% faster with bare hands
Bronx
50% more damage dealt with bare hands
Andalusia
10% less Piercing and Shock damage taken; 5% more Slash and Heat damage taken
Grog
10% less Slash and Heat damage taken; 5% more Blunt and Cold damage taken
Daiquiri
10% less Blunt and Cold damage taken; 5% more Piercing and Shock damage taken
Moonshine
heals Caustic damage
Amasec
equivalent to Arithrazine
Hooch
equivalent to Ephedrine
Moscow Mule
10% less Poison and Radiation damage taken
Pan-Galactic Gargle Blaster
10% less Blunt, Slash, and Piercing damage taken
Erika Surprise
15% less Heat, Cold, and Shock damage taken
### Hard Brew These drinks require a bit more work to mix.
Xeno Basher
10% faster attacks with melee weapons
Irish Car Bomb
20% more damage dealt with melee weapons
Driest Martini
15% less Slash, Piercing, and Blunt damage taken
Syndicate Bomb
25% more interaction (DoAfter) speed
Radler
heal Slash, Piercing, and Blunt damage while in critical condition
Allies Cocktail
heal Caustic, Heat, and Cold damage while in critical condition
Irish Coffee
10% increased movement speed
Budget Insuls
insulation from shocks
The Sun Also Rises
low-light vision
Alien Brain Hemorrhage
heals Genetic damage
Rubberneck
equivalent to Desoxyephedrine
Electric Shark
ignore slowdown
### Expert Brew Concocting these drinks is best left to the talents of a seasoned Bartender and/or Priest.
Painkiller
100% less Bloodloss damage taken
Irish Bool
rapidly recover from stun and knockdown; 90% less Stamina damage taken
Crush Depth
no damage from pressure effects
B52
25% less damage taken from explosions
Bacchus Blessing
equivalent to Omnizine
The Highlander
raises critical threshold to 219 damage (death threshold is still 220)
